questions.question: Evaluate $\int_0^\infty \frac{1}{(x^2+1)^2} \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Use trig substitution $x=\tan\theta$, $dx=\sec^2\theta d\theta$. The integral becomes $\int_0^{\pi/2} \cos^2\theta d\theta = \frac{\pi}{4}$.

questions.question: Determine convergence: $\int_1^\infty \frac{\sin x}{x^2} \, dx$.
questions.explanation: $|\frac{\sin x}{x^2}| \le \frac{1}{x^2}$ and $\int_1^\infty \frac{1}{x^2}dx$ converges, so it converges absolutely.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int_{-\infty}^\infty \frac{x}{x^2+1} \, dx$.
questions.explanation: The integral diverges because $\int_0^\infty \frac{x}{x^2+1}dx$ diverges logarithmically.

questions.question: Determine convergence: $\int_0^\infty \frac{x^2}{x^4+1} \, dx$.
questions.explanation: $\frac{x^2}{x^4+1} \sim \frac{1}{x^2}$ as $x\to\infty$, and near 0 it is continuous, so converges.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int_0^1 \frac{dx}{x (\ln x)^2}$.
questions.explanation: Substitute $u=\ln x$, $du=dx/x$, then $\int_{-\infty}^0 u^{-2} du$ diverges at $u=0$.

questions.question: Determine convergence: $\int_0^\infty \frac{1}{x^{3/2}} \, dx$ (split at 1).
questions.explanation: $\int_0^1 x^{-3/2}dx$ diverges (p=1.5>1), so the total diverges.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered all improper integral concepts.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the p-test and comparison tests.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on recognizing types of improper integrals.
scoreMessages.0: Review improper integrals and convergence criteria.
